On "in a parcel of ground which Jacob bought of the sons of Hamor the father of Shechem for an hundred pieces of silver: and it became the inheritance of the children of Joseph," the reformed Calvin and grammatical-historical note the inheritance. Calvin and Gill note Joseph buried in the very ground Jacob bought, the long-deferred promise kept to the letter, the patriarch's faith looking beyond death to the inheritance, the believer dying in faith of what he does not see (the faith of the patriarchs, Hebrews 11:13; Hebrews 11:22) — the figure (the believer who dies in faith of the resurrection and the inheritance); the providence (the burial, the death of Eleazar to follow, vv.33). The verse displays Joseph's bones buried at Shechem in the ground Jacob bought.

The witnesses agree the substance: Joseph's bones, carried from Egypt by his dying charge, were buried at last in the promised land in the ground Jacob bought (Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill). The verse bears its weight as the crux of faith beyond death: Joseph's bones buried, the patriarch dying in faith of what he did not see (Hebrews 11:13), the bones a standing testimony of the promise through the wilderness (Hebrews 11:22), a figure of the believer who dies in faith of the resurrection and the inheritance. The verse displays the faith-beyond-death crux. Joseph's bones buried at Shechem in the ground Jacob bought display the crux: the bones of Joseph, which the children of Israel brought up out of Egypt, buried they in Shechem... and it became the inheritance of the children of Joseph (Genesis 50:25; Hebrews 11:13; Hebrews 11:22) — the faith that looks beyond death to the promise, Joseph's dying charge fulfilled at the last, the long-deferred word kept and the patriarch at rest in the promised land. The verse displays the death and burial of Eleazar the priest (vv.33).
33:
"And Eleazar the son of Aaron died; and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim." The very brief verse notes the death and burial of Eleazar the priest, closing the book. The grammatical voices note the close. Keil and Delitzsch and Gill note that "Eleazar the son of Aaron died; and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim" — Eleazar the priest died and was buried in Ephraim, the book closing with the passing of the leaders; the and Eleazar the son of Aaron died (Eleazar died); the and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim (buried in Ephraim); the figure (the death of the high priest closing the generation of the conquest, the leaders passing; the truth that with Joshua, Joseph's burial, and Eleazar, the book closes the age of the conquest, the generation gathered to their rest; the figure of the passing of the faithful generation, the work done and the leaders at rest, "the fathers, where are they?" Zechariah 1:5; the death of Eleazar; the close of the book, vv.29). The verse closes the book of Joshua.

On "And Eleazar the son of Aaron died," the witnesses note the death. Gill notes the death of Eleazar the priest (the close, Zechariah 1:5) — the death (Eleazar died).

On "and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim," the grammatical-historical Ellicott and grammatical-historical note the burial: "buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim" — Eleazar buried in Ephraim (the rest); the figure (the passing of the faithful generation); the providence (the close, the book of Judges to follow). The verse displays the death and burial of Eleazar, closing the book.

The very brief comment matches the very brief verse. The witnesses agree: the close — "Eleazar the son of Aaron died; and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son," the close (Keil, Gill); "And Eleazar the son of Aaron died," the death (Zechariah 1:5, Gill); "and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim," the burial (Ellicott, grammatical-historical). The verse displays the close. The verse marks Eleazar: died. The verse displays the burial: in Ephraim. The verse displays the generation: passing. The verse displays the book: closed. The close displays the passing of the faithful generation, the work done. The verse displays the priest: at rest. The verse displays the leaders: gathered. The verse displays the age: ended. The death and burial of Eleazar closing the book display the passing of the faithful generation: Eleazar the son of Aaron died; and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim (Zechariah 1:5) — Eleazar the high priest dying and buried in Ephraim, the book closing with the passing of the leaders of the conquest, the faithful generation gathered to their rest. The verse closes the book of Joshua, before the days of the Judges.
